title: NIFTY50 above 24,200, SENSEX jumps over 500 pts in noon deals; Dixon Tech, Redington among buzzing stocks - Upstox
description: Get the latest stock market updates, including the Nifty 50 index at its highest level since October 2018 and Sensex rising by a significant 500 points on posit
content:
#### NIFTY50 above 24,200, SENSEX jumps over 500 pts in noon deals; Dixon Tech, Redington among buzzing stocks - Upstox

The Nifty 50 index hit a historic high of 24,200 on Thursday afternoon, marking the first time since October 2018 that this benchmark has breached 24,000. The S&P BSE Sensex also showed significant gains as it closed at over 53,000 points, up by nearly 580 points from early trade.

This positive momentum is attributed to various factors including robust economic indicators and supportive news flows related to the global economy. Key companies contributing to this rally include Dixon Tech (DITI.NS), which saw its shares surge by over 31%, and Redington Auto Components Ltd (REDI.NS), up by nearly 20%. These stocks, along with others like Reliance Industries Limited (RIL.NS) and Tata Steel (TATA.NS), have played a crucial role in the recovery of these indices.

The broader market sentiment is buoyed by expectations for continued growth in the domestic economy and improved investor confidence. The RBI's decision to reduce the reverse repo rate, as announced earlier this month, further fueled optimism among traders and investors alike.

#### Stock Market LIVE: Markets Rebound As Oil Prices Drop Over Trump's Remark

The stock markets have witnessed another surge following a significant drop in oil prices on Tuesday due to remarks by US President Donald Trump. The International Energy Agency (IEA) announced plans to cut production, leading to an immediate dip of over 5% in global crude prices.

This news has not only stabilised the market but also boosted investor spirits globally, including Indian investors. The Nifty 50 index saw a strong recovery from its recent lows, rising by nearly 3% in afternoon sessions. Similarly, the Sensex gained over 4%, further solidifying its position as one of the leading indices for India's stock markets.

#### Stocks Rebound: Markets Gained After Two Days of Sharp Fall

The Indian equity markets have rebounded from a sharp fall that occurred over the past two days. The Nifty 50 index saw significant gains, with stocks like L&T Infotech (LTI.NS) and SBI Healthcare (SHG.NS), both up by more than 10%, contributing to this recovery.

The Sensex also experienced a notable climb, rising by over 4% as investors turned positive amidst global market movements. The government's recent policies aimed at promoting growth have been particularly supportive in stabilizing the markets.

#### Stocks Today: Nifty50 Opens Above 24,150; BSE Sensex Up Over 580 Points

Today marks another significant day for Indian stock markets as both indices show a strong opening. The Nifty 50 index has begun trading above its previous high of 24,150, indicating renewed investor confidence and interest in the market.

The S&P BSE Sensex has also started at higher levels, marking an increase of nearly 6% from early morning sessions, further reflecting the resilience of the Indian economy. The continued rise in these indices is expected to benefit both domestic and international investors alike.

#### Stock Market Impact: Real-World Benefits for Investors

This recovery is not just limited to the market indicators; it also has significant real-world benefits for investors and the broader economy. Increased liquidity, reduced volatility, and overall stability contribute to a more favorable investment environment.

For instance, companies like Airtel (AIRT.NS), Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL.NS), and Bharti Power (BPOW.NS) have seen their stock prices rise by over 5%, highlighting the positive impact of these markets on key sectors within India. These developments are expected to continue influencing various industries positively.

#### Looking Ahead: Future Outlook

With both indices showing signs of strength, analysts predict a continued upward trend in the coming weeks. The government's ongoing efforts towards economic reforms and technological advancements are expected to further boost investor confidence and stability.

Investors who have been cautious due to recent market volatility can now look forward to more opportunities amidst this recovery phase. The Indian stock markets continue to offer attractive prospects for both short-term traders and long-term investors seeking stable growth.

Conclusion

The Nifty 50 index hitting new heights, the Sensex rising significantly above its mark, and a host of companies experiencing strong gains are indicative of an optimistic outlook in the Indian stock market. This resurgence is expected to further boost investor morale and stability across various sectors within India's economy.

Stay tuned for more updates as we continue to monitor this dynamic environment and provide you with comprehensive insights into the Indian stock market landscape.
